abstract:
The electronic journal *Economic Sociology* has been in continuous publication since 2000. It was established by Vadim Radaev, who continues to serve as Editor-in-Chief, and has been officially co-founded by the National Research University Higher School of Economics since 2007.

As a specialized academic publication, the journal is dedicated to upholding and promoting international research standards within the Russian context. It provides a platform for both established and emerging scholars—from Russia and abroad—to present contemporary research that captures the current state of economic sociology. By informing the professional community of new publications and innovative research projects, the journal also seeks to actively mentor and integrate younger colleagues into the global academic dialogue.

*Economic Sociology* prioritizes several key thematic areas: major theoretical paradigms, the sociological analysis of markets and organizations, the social and economic strategies of individuals and households, and the dynamics of the informal economy. Furthermore, the journal welcomes contributions from interdisciplinary intersections, including new institutional economics, anthropology, economic psychology, and other fields that offer valuable insights for economic sociologists. 

The journal is published bimonthly (five times a year) and provides permanent, free open access to all materials. Every submission undergoes a rigorous double-blind peer-review process to ensure the highest academic quality.
